REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, TO PRESERVE THE STATUS QUO (PRIOR TO PARTIES INSTALLING AND CHANGING INTERIOR LOCKS) PARTIES ARE TO HAVE SHARED POSSESSION OF THE TWO BEDROOMS AND THE HALLWAY CLOSET SUBJECT TO THIS ACTION. INTERNAL LOCKS, IF ANY STILL REMAIN, SHALL BE REMOVED. PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER IN GOOD FAITH REGARDING DEFENDANT'S REASONABLE ACCESS TO THE CLOSETS AND TO REPORT OF THEIR AGREEMENT AT THE TIME OF THE HEARING. ALSO, TO PRESERVE THE STATUS QUO, NO RENTAL PAYMENTS SHALL BE POSTED. COUNSEL ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING A PROPOSED ORDER. Order To Show Casue Re: Injunction
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Thomas Droham, 415-538-3333 for Plaintiff Ellen James.
Karen Uchiyama, 415-563-9301 for Defendant Brent Marsh.

Argued by counsel for both sides. The Court rules as follows: Order To Show Casue Re: Injunction is GRANTED as follows: To preserve the status quo (prior to parties installing and changing interior locks) parties are to have shared possession of the two bedrooms and the hallway closet subject to this action. Internal locks, if any still remain, shall be removed. Parties are to meet and confer in good faith regarding Defendant's reasonable access to the closets and to report of their agreement at the time of the hearing. Also, to preserve the status quo, no rental payments shall be posted. Counsel ordered to meet and confer regarding a proposed order. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
